items.title: Refunds but no price caps for ripped-off older Australians
items.summary: Support at Home program price caps, due to start in July, have been deferred indefinitely. Hundreds of thousands of older people rely on the federal government program, which provides help with cleaning, showering and clinical supports like nursing.

items.title: Reserve Bank worries about inflation pressures building, risk of a recession
items.summary: The Reserve Bank is growing increasingly anxious about price pressures building in the economy and its chief economist has raised the risk of a recession in post-budget remarks.

items.title: Why Cuba is warning US military action could spark 'bloodbath' after months of rising tensions
items.summary: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez says Cuba's right to defend itself should not be used by the US as justification for a potential war.

items.title: TISM fined for $18,000 in damage to Sydney Opera House ahead of tour
items.summary: The anarchic, ARIA-winning group has been hit with a hefty fine by the Sydney Opera House before its first national tour in more than 30 years.

items.title: Why Queensland rookie Sam Walker is the great unknown of State of Origin I
items.summary: Sam Walker has always seemed like a player destined for the highest levels of rugby league, but ahead of his State of Origin debut, it's impossible to know what comes next for the new Queensland halfback.

items.title: Two people die from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking NT health alert
items.summary: Two people have died from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking a health alert for the rare mosquito-borne disease across the Northern Territory.

items.title: Eligibility expanded for Queensland's free nasal spray flu vaccine program
items.summary: With stocks set to expire in August, the FluMist nasal spray has been made free for all children over the age of two.

items.title: Key takeaways from Sam Altman and Elon Musk's bitter legal battle over OpenAI
items.summary: A legal showdown between two of the tech world's most powerful men has reached its end. Here's what we learned throughout the trial.

items.title: Network 10 to go off air in country SA and NSW as WIN Television deal lapses
items.summary: The end of a broadcast deal between Network 10 and Win Television is set to leave three regional markets without access to multiple channels.

items.title: Scientists investigate mystery illness paralysing and killing hundreds of magpies
items.summary: Researchers say they are still far from identifying the cause of a deadly paralytic illness spreading through populations of the iconic Australian birds.

items.title: Tasmania government apologises for display of autopsy body parts in museum without consent
items.summary: A formal apology is given in Tasmania's parliament for the past practice of taking human specimens from autopsies and displaying them in a museum without the knowledge of family members.

items.title: 'Nowhere for me to exist': Growing up as a biracial child in Australia
items.summary: KA was "stripped" of her culture as a child and has been on a journey to reclaim her connection to her Filipina heritage.

items.title: NSW Police Association pushes for night courts to ease pressure on frontline officers
items.summary: The NSW Police Association makes its case for night courts at its biennial conference in Wollongong.

items.title: Man, 47, faces court charged with murdering wife and sons in Campbelltown home
items.summary: The 47-year-old has faced court charged with what police describe as the "particularly violent" murder of his wife and sons in Sydney's south-west.

items.title: Hawthorn 'extremely disappointed' after AFL forces club to end Tasmanian partnership
items.summary: Hawthorn is less than pleased after being forced to cease playing home games in Tasmania ahead of the Devils' AFL entry.

items.title: Neymar included in Brazil's FIFA World Cup squad despite three-year international hiatus
items.summary: Carlo Ancelotti includes Brazil's all-time leading goalscorer in his first FIFA World Cup squad.

items.title: Australians detained after Israel intercepted Gaza-bound flotilla
items.summary: Israeli forces detain 11 Australians after its navy intercepts ships seeking to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza, flotilla organisers say.

items.title: Northern Territory diphtheria outbreak spreads across borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia
items.summary: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

items.title: Ebola treatment centres to open in Congo as American doctor diagnosed
items.summary: The cash-strapped World Health Organization is sending a team of experts to the Democratic Republic of the Congo as hundreds of cases of Ebola are suspected.

items.title: WHO Director-General opens 79th World Health Assembly
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Long-time Nationals MP Tim Bull decides not to contest 2026 Victorian election
items.summary: Tim Bull has announced he will not contest the November election, after representing Gippsland East for the National Party for 16 years.

items.title: Central Queensland ambulance drama prompts push for no-name roads investigation
items.summary: When Chris Stehbens spied a lost ambulance driving up and down his road during a Triple Zero (000) emergency response, he was not surprised by the struggle to find the right address.

items.title: Arsenal edges closer to Premier League title after nervy win as VAR spares Kai Havertz red card
items.summary: Kai Havertz emerges Arsenal's hero after scoring the winner against Burnley, and is spared a sending-off for a horror tackle.

items.title: Bernard Tomic edges closer to shock French Open return as fellow Australians struggle in qualifiers
items.summary: Bernard Tomic is just two wins from reaching his first main draw of the French Open since 2019 after being the only Australian to enjoy success in the qualifiers.

items.title: Collins-class submarine overhaul scaled back in 'pivot to reduce risk' — as it happened
items.summary: Changes are being made to massive program aiming to upgrade and extend the life of Australia's aging fleet of Collins Class submarines, Defence Minister Richard Marles has confirmed.

items.title: Prime minister knocks back $94 million proposal to help flooded farmers restock
items.summary: Widespread flooding in north-west Queensland killed more than 90,000 head of livestock this year, but farmers will have to go it alone if they want to rebuild.

items.title: Stranded school students rescued from national park as deluge drenches Gold Coast
items.summary: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim.

items.title: Pat Cummins propels Sunrisers Hyderabad into IPL playoffs after win over Chennai Super Kings
items.summary: Pat Cummins delivers when his team needs it most with crucial wickets as Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Chennai Super Kings.

items.title: Motor neurone disease research to begin in Toowoomba after community fundraising effort
items.summary: Natalie has seen her body and speech deteriorate; she has lost her independence and dignity — but she says new research into motor neurone disease has given her hope for those diagnosed in the future.

items.title: Kylie Minogue on career-spanning doco, Michael Hutchence, Nick Cave and the AFL
items.summary: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.

items.title: Granny flats allowed again in parts of Sydney's south-west near Western Sydney airport
items.summary: The NSW government will permit secondary dwellings such as granny flats in some areas affected by noise from Western Sydney International Airport.

items.title: Queensland councils use specialist task forces to crack down on illegal waste dumping
items.summary: While Queensland councils cleaned up enough illegally dumped waste to fill more than 1,200 garbage trucks, investigators say they are getting better at catching those doing the wrong thing.

items.title: Shallow burials, landfill: Where tonnes of Tasmania's dead salmon went
items.summary: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

items.title: Where tonnes of Tasmania's dead salmon went
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Donald Trump says he has postponed a planned new strike on Iran
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says an attack on Iran that was "scheduled for tomorrow" has been postponed at the request of Gulf state leaders.

items.title: Tasmania's treasurer Eric Abetz talks a big game on debt. But how far is he willing to cut the budget?
items.summary: The lead-up to state budget day is normally a time for flashy funding announcements. But as Tasmania stares down the barrel of $7 billion of debt, things are looking a bit bleaker.

items.title: The networks we rely on face growing risk from natural disasters
items.summary: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.

items.title: People using super for weight-loss surgery, but those looking overseas face risks
items.summary: Weight-loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.

items.title: Young investors accept 'party had to end' but rethink financial plans after budget CGT reforms
items.summary: Some young Australians locked out of the property market due to unaffordable housing have pursued ETFs and "rentvesting" as a way to build financial security. Here's what they make of the budget's CGT reforms.

items.title: Should the tax system reward those who can afford to take risks?
items.summary: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that has allowed many of those people to take those risks.

items.title: How to deal with money stress in your relationship
items.summary: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits and how they respond to financial pressure.

items.title: The Talented Mr F follows two filmmakers trying to find their stolen movie
items.summary: What do you do when your stolen film wins a slew of awards for the thief? In the case of these filmmakers, you travel across the globe to hunt him down.

items.title: Australians share the moment they realised gambling had changed their lives forever
items.summary: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.

items.title: ABC Timelines quiz: Guess the correct order of these famous movie quotes from the 2000s
items.summary: The ABC Timelines quiz is back to test your knowledge of some of the most iconic movie quotes of the 2000s.

items.title: Two Americans arrested over man breaking into enclosure of Punch the monkey
items.summary: One of the men was detained by a zookeeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

items.title: Iran International journalist Pouria Zaratifoukolaei stabbed in London at behest of Iran, court told
items.summary: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

items.title: Spanish PM's Socialists party on notice after historic defeat in stronghold
items.summary: Voters in the region of Andalusia have sent the ruling Spain's Socialists yet another warning, adding to concerns Pedro Sánchez's government could suffer an electoral wipe-out in a nationwide vote.

items.title: Man charged with murder after woman and two young children found dead in south-west Sydney home
items.summary: A man, 47, has been charged after three people were found dead in a home in Sydney's south-west in what police allege is a domestic violence-related incident.

items.title: NT police officer on trial charged with attempting to pervert the course of justice
items.summary: A jury has heard police officer Karol Jarentowski repeatedly attempted to pressure his former partner into dropping aggravated assault charges against him, including at one point threatening her child support.

items.title: Should we be worried about the latest Ebola outbreak?
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Almost 1400 smelter jobs at risk in Port Pirie
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: From retirement to record breaker, Romelda Aiken-George keeps reinventing herself
items.summary: Romelda Aiken-George is supposed to be retired. Instead, the 37-year-old goal shooter is breaking all kinds of records and reinventing herself in her 19th season. After her 255th national league game on the weekend, she reveals the secret to her longevity.

items.title: Clare Wright wins top prize at NSW Literary Awards for 'powerful' history book Näku Dhäruk The Bark Petitions
items.summary: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.

items.title: How WA's approach to shark mitigation has evolved over decades after another fatal attack
items.summary: The approach to tackling shark attacks has changed dramatically since the 2013 catch-and-kill policy, with a focus on shark tagging and tracking. But it's never enough to stop fatal attacks.

items.title: Israeli forces intercept Gaza flotilla with Australians on board, organisers say
items.summary: The organisers of a flotilla of ships trying to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza say Israel's navy is intercepting the group off the coast of Cyprus.

items.title: Free public transport, extended timetables among solutions to NSW live music scene, inquiry told
items.summary: An inquiry into the state of live music in NSW hears from industry stakeholders looking to service younger audiences sprawled across Greater Sydney.

items.title: Scott Pendlebury not looking too far into the future as AFL/VFL games record looms
items.summary: Collingwood champion Pendlebury will break the AFL games record against West Coast but hasn't pondered his future beyond then.

items.title: US Africa Command conducts additional strikes against Islamic State militants in Nigeria
items.summary: The US Africa Command announced the strikes days after Trump claimed to have killed the group's second-in-command.

items.title: Government blocks private colleges from offering new courses as part of student visa crackdown
items.summary: Australia suspends private colleges from applying to offer new courses to international students as part of a crackdown on abuse in the student visa system.

items.title: Cull of 20,000 native birds at Newcastle Waters cattle station will not go ahead after parent company intervened
items.summary: A cull of 20,000 native birds on a Northern Territory cattle station will not be going ahead.

items.title: Trial begins, pitting pianist Jayson Gillham against Melbourne Symphony Orchestra over his Gaza comments
items.summary: Pianist Jayson Gillham is suing the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra in the Federal Court over political discrimination after comments he made about Gaza on stage during a 2024 performance.

items.title: Treasurer says 'death tax' claims are a scare campaign, 'pure and simple'
items.summary: The Coalition seizes on the federal government's broken tax promises, claiming a "death tax" has been sneaked into changes to trusts passed on through inheritance. The treasurer says that is a scare campaign.

items.title: Man jailed for assaulting off-duty police officer in Newtown has sentence reduced
items.summary: Koby Weaver, 21, was sentenced last month to two years' jail, after he ran into a brawl between two of his friends and two off-duty officers.

items.title: Make the most of cheap and versatile tinned beans with these recipes
items.summary: From vegetarian chilli to hearty soup, these easy recipes might convince you to add beans to your regular dinner rotation.
